findings.title: How much GPU time could move off the GPU
findings.statement: Time-weighted substrate verdicts over every profiled kernel: this share of the GPU second lands on a PiM/ISP substrate rather than GPU-keep — the measured size of the offload opportunity, per workload.

findings.title: Verdicts that flip between workloads
findings.statement: The same kernel can deserve a different substrate under a different dataset/feature toggle — the dynamic metrics (DRAM SoL, sectors/request, footprint) move enough to change the decision. These kernels need runtime or per-product placement; each flip is recorded with the metric that moved most.

findings.title: Every byte of DRAM traffic has a name
findings.statement: The two-pass join (TaggedAllocator source journal × NVBit trace) resolves each kernel's memory accesses into shared-tile / register-spill / global space and names the data structure behind the global traffic. The top tag is unanimous across all 27 sequences for nearly every kernel.
findings.unit: kernels with a unanimous data-structure tag

findings.title: The loop-closure scan's DRAM traffic is mostly scratch
findings.statement: The dominant SLAM kernel's DRAM traffic is register SPILL (compiler scratch), not data: the 'coalesced' signal earlier traces showed was the spill stream. Space-filtered, the actual data accesses are a scattered gather (23–30 sectors/warp) — counters and traces agree. Implication: a register-pressure fix attacks most of this kernel's traffic; the remaining gather is the PiM-scatter candidate.

evidence.label: open st_track in the explorer (evidence panel)

findings.title: The bottleneck classes are properties of kernels, not runs
findings.statement: Across 27 sequences × 4 datasets the modal bottleneck class per kernel is highly stable; the residual flips are the physically meaningful L2-capacity crossover (room-scale fits, street-scale doesn't), not noise.

findings.title: The taxonomy is discovered, not asserted
findings.statement: Unsupervised k-means over the pooled 27-sequence feature cloud prefers the same cluster count as the hand-built G-classes — the decision tree is the labeling, clustering is the independent validation.

findings.title: Profiling does not change the answer
findings.statement: Every profiled run re-computes trajectory accuracy vs ground truth; the delta against the un-profiled run is the neutrality check. Deterministic modes are bit-identical under the profilers; every flagged CHECK was classified benign (mono scale ambiguity, known nondeterminism, stale baselines) — none is instrumentation.

evidence.label: plain vs profiled APE, every variant (interactive)

findings.title: Full-matrix coverage
findings.statement: The complete config matrix — every base × pipeline kind × feature toggle — ran accuracy + nsys + ncu (+ NVBit where marked) on locked clocks. Every cell is resumable, ledgered, and lands in the evidence explorer.

evidence.label: filter the run selector to any config

findings.title: Locked-clock measurement floor
findings.statement: All numbers are taken at locked GPU clocks (1620/7001 MHz, persistence on): 5-repeat coefficient of variation 0.14% median vs 49.6% unlocked. Ceilings are measured, not quoted: 205 GB/s DRAM, 5445 GFLOP/s FP32 — these are the roof lines drawn in the explorer.

evidence.label: open a locked-clock study in the explorer

methodology.name: 1 · Capture
methodology.what: nsys timeline (stages, full run) + ncu curated metric sets (windowed kernel replay) + NVBit mem_trace (windowed binary instrumentation) on locked clocks; every run's QoR recorded.

methodology.name: 2 · Screen
methodology.what: per-kernel time shares from the timeline; SoL pair, occupancy, stall taxonomy, coalescing fingerprint from the counters — the feature row every later step consumes.

methodology.name: 3 · Classify
methodology.what: GPU-adapted DAMOV decision tree (G0–G7) over stated thresholds, ±25% sensitivity-stressed; independently validated by k-means on the pooled feature cloud.

methodology.name: 4 · Attribute
methodology.what: two-pass join: TaggedAllocator source journal × NVBit trace → memory-space split (shared / spill / global) + the named data structure behind every global byte.

methodology.name: 5 · Validate
methodology.what: accuracy/QoR vs ground truth under every profiler (neutrality), cross-sequence class stability, threshold sensitivity — the checks that make the numbers defensible.

methodology.name: 6 · Verdict
methodology.what: class × persistence × affinity rules → the substrate call per kernel (GPU-keep / GPU+layout-fix / PiM-near-bank / PiM-scatter / ISP / CPU), with the driving reason recorded; flips across workloads flag placement that must be dynamic.
